title: How a tutor learned to change course

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for The average stayed flat. We wanted to know what happened at the stuck point.: a long black average-slope ruler crossing an off-white Swiss grid while one dec...
images.caption: Where we began: Paper 2.0 compared tutors prompted in different ways.
images.section_heading: The average stayed flat. We wanted to know what happened at the stuck point.
images.visual_metaphor: a long black average-slope ruler crossing an off-white Swiss grid while one decisive lesson hinge falls just outside its frame; a single red -18-degree cut marks the missed turning point beside the paired tutor and learner figures

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for We made the turning point the whole experiment.: a tutoring table staged as three crisp beats on a black ruled grid—block, turn, checked result—with a red ch...
images.caption: Changing the question: The challenge was to make each beat visible without claiming we could infer a hidden mental state.
images.section_heading: We made the turning point the whole experiment.
images.visual_metaphor: a tutoring table staged as three crisp beats on a black ruled grid—block, turn, checked result—with a red checker stamp landing only on the final beat and the tutor and learner visible throughout

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for The tutor cannot give away the answer. It has to help the learner reach it.: a proof map of black nodes and sharp rules that the learner crosses one supporte...
images.caption: A lesson we can check: As the learner establishes each fact, the remaining distance to the answer gets shorter.
images.section_heading: The tutor cannot give away the answer. It has to help the learner reach it.
images.visual_metaphor: a proof map of black nodes and sharp rules that the learner crosses one supported step at a time; one red path advances toward a small D→0 register while the answer node remains covered by an opaque black square

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for “Be gentler” did not help. A clear rule did.: the same black rhetorical mask repeating down a rigid film strip; a pale manner note leaves the strip unchanged...
images.caption: The first surprise: Left to itself, the tutor fell into a rut.
images.section_heading: “Be gentler” did not help. A clear rule did.
images.visual_metaphor: the same black rhetorical mask repeating down a rigid film strip; a pale manner note leaves the strip unchanged, while one red device card cuts across it and rotates the next mask into a genuinely different move

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for A small internal check made the tutor break its own habit.: an empty director chair outside the square frame while a compact black-and-red gate inside the tu...
images.caption: Moving the rule inside: An outside director could make the tutor name its move, but we wanted the tutor to carry the rule itself.
images.section_heading: A small internal check made the tutor break its own habit.
images.visual_metaphor: an empty director chair outside the square frame while a compact black-and-red gate inside the tutor outline catches the third repeated gesture and reroutes it before the speech arrow reaches the learner

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for The tutor did not need more skill. It needed to notice the loss.: two matched tutor panels facing the same learner ledger as one established node fades: the...
images.caption: The learner who forgets: The simulated learner usually said enough for the tutor to infer what was happening.
images.section_heading: The tutor did not need more skill. It needed to notice the loss.
images.visual_metaphor: two matched tutor panels facing the same learner ledger as one established node fades: the first has an open evidence window and restores the node with a red repair bracket; the second has that channel replaced by a blank black square and cannot target the repair

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for One sensible early clue broke the lesson. One small rule repaired it.: a clue calendar drawn as a clean Swiss grid: one red clue tile moves a single cell too...
images.caption: Handing over the calendar: We then let the tutor decide when to release clues.
images.section_heading: One sensible early clue broke the lesson. One small rule repaired it.
images.visual_metaphor: a clue calendar drawn as a clean Swiss grid: one red clue tile moves a single cell too early and breaks the black proof line; a compact next-step rule snaps the missing fact back into place before the line continues

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for Two pacing rules helped. Which one helped depended on the shape of the lesson.: a split black-rule composition with two sensors labelled V and H: V reads the...
images.caption: Does the rule travel: Without help, the tutor's timing was unreliable: it completed only 4 of 10 proofs across two replication sets.
images.section_heading: Two pacing rules helped. Which one helped depended on the shape of the lesson.
images.visual_metaphor: a split black-rule composition with two sensors labelled V and H: V reads the conversation surface, H reads the explicit proof-state depth; each red selection path succeeds on one small lesson geometry and stops at a black boundary on another, with no overall winner

images.alt: Editorial cartoon for The tutor can govern parts of its conduct. The claim stays deliberately small.: three square evidence cards locked to one grid—a check, a boundary bracket, a...
images.caption: What we can honestly say: The tutor can govern parts of its conduct.
images.section_heading: The tutor can govern parts of its conduct. The claim stays deliberately small.
images.visual_metaphor: three square evidence cards locked to one grid—a check, a boundary bracket, and an open ring—while a heavy red stamp marks the narrow claim CONDUCT ONLY; the paired tutor and learner remain outside any imagined mind-reading apparatus
